instances.task_instruction:
You are solving a workforce scheduling task. Fill the 5x2 grid with worker ids.
This workforce scheduling plan has 5 work days as rows and 2 shift slots to staff per day as columns.
Each cell must use exactly one candidate id from that slot.

Across the whole grid, the plan must satisfy these global constraints:
(1) the total hourly cost of all workers in the whole grid must be at most 760.
(2) the total skill of all workers in the whole grid must be at least 16.
(3) there must be at least 6 workers in the whole grid whose experience is at least 12.
(4) the same department can appear at most 7 times in the whole grid.

For the row constraints:
For work day 0:
(1) the total hourly cost of all workers in work day 0 must be at most 168.
(2) the total skill of all workers in work day 0 must be at least 1.
(3) the total reliability of all workers in work day 0 must be at least 2.

For work day 1:
(1) the total hourly cost of all workers in work day 1 must be at most 200.
(2) the total skill of all workers in work day 1 must be at least 1.
(3) the total reliability of all workers in work day 1 must be at least 4.

For work day 2:
(1) the total hourly cost of all workers in work day 2 must be at most 108.
(2) the total skill of all workers in work day 2 must be at least 3.
(3) the total reliability of all workers in work day 2 must be at least 3.

For work day 3:
(1) the total hourly cost of all workers in work day 3 must be at most 180.
(2) the total skill of all workers in work day 3 must be at least 8.
(3) the total reliability of all workers in work day 3 must be at least 3.

For work day 4:
(1) the total hourly cost of all workers in work day 4 must be at most 75.
(2) the total skill of all workers in work day 4 must be at least 3.
(3) the total reliability of all workers in work day 4 must be at least 5.

For the column constraints:
For shift slot 0:
(1) the total hourly cost of all workers in shift slot 0 must be at most 346.
(2) the total skill of all workers in shift slot 0 must be at least 9.
(3) the total overtime capacity of all workers in shift slot 0 must be at least 32.

For shift slot 1:
(1) the total hourly cost of all workers in shift slot 1 must be at most 331.
(2) the total skill of all workers in shift slot 1 must be at least 11.
(3) the total overtime capacity of all workers in shift slot 1 must be at least 18.

Each slot also has its own local constraints, but those slot-specific limits must be looked up from the dataset/database for that slot before deciding which candidate id to use.
Return only a 5x2 list of worker ids.
